jokes.text: Robot, kick him in the knees. Bears have weak knees. He should vibrate, 'cause the robot's full of radiation.
jokes.setup: Writer trying to direct an action sequence for a robot vs bears sketch
jokes.punchline: Treating ridiculous premise with complete seriousness, including bear anatomy expertise
jokes.explanation: Establishes the absurdist tone of the show - treating nonsense with earnest expertise

jokes.text: Save a little money for the rest of us. You can't spend a lot of money on bear suits that are only gonna be seen for 25 seconds.
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on TV production economics mixed with bear suit absurdity
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock meta-humor - self-aware about TV production while maintaining character consistency

jokes.text: You're trying to bring logic to the robot-bear sketch?
jokes.setup: Writers arguing about realism in their sketch
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the absurdity of applying logic to something inherently illogical
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice - Liz's exasperation at creative chaos

jokes.text: Who did my sudoku puzzle? I have been looking forward to this puzzle all morning.
jokes.punchline: In the midst of bear suit arguments, she's concerned about sudoku
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Liz's need for small personal pleasures amid chaos

jokes.text: I never have a free moment, Jack. Never, ever.
jokes.setup: Jack asking for a free moment
jokes.punchline: Liz's dramatic over-emphasis on her busy-ness
jokes.explanation: Basic character beat, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: How does your life improve? Do the hands have money in them?
jokes.punchline: Liz's materialistic/practical interpretation of spiritual healing
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - takes spiritual concept and makes it purely transactional

jokes.text: I can tell from your stress level that you have not been touched in quite some time... Not caressed, not massaged, not even groped on the subway.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from romantic touch to subway harassment as equally valid human contact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack voice - clinical analysis that becomes increasingly inappropriate

jokes.text: I would think that a single woman's biggest worry would be choking to death alone in her apartment.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Liz's personal life priorities
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact delivery of a morbid assumption about single women
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack - delivers dark observation with corporate efficiency and total confidence

jokes.text: Poker night? Who plays? Really, that's all you got out of that?
jokes.setup: Liz gives lengthy speech about inappropriate workplace behavior
jokes.punchline: Jack ignores her entire argument to focus on poker invitation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Jack's selective hearing perfectly executed

jokes.text: I bluffed. Yes, I am coming.
jokes.punchline: Liz admitting she was lying but then immediately giving in anyway
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's inability to maintain boundaries with Jack

jokes.text: Oh, my God, isn't Jack friends with Tom DeLay? Am I going on a date with Tom DeLay?
jokes.setup: Learning about Jack's friend named Thomas
jokes.punchline: Jumping to worst-case scenario about Republican politician
jokes.explanation: Good political humor that reveals Liz's liberal anxieties

jokes.text: But if it is Tom DeLay and you do go home with him, be prepared for it to get weird.
jokes.setup: Discussion about potentially dating Tom DeLay
jokes.punchline: Implication that sex with the politician would be strange
jokes.explanation: Decent political humor but relatively predictable

jokes.text: I used to play all kinds of poker with my aunts... Crazy 7, Albuquerque Freak-Out, One-Card Stud. Yeah, I don't think those are real games.
jokes.punchline: Made-up game names followed by immediate deflation
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy moment - confident in completely made-up expertise

jokes.text: Whatever. Get ready to get took.
jokes.setup: Being told his poker games aren't real
jokes.punchline: Dismissing correction with confident grammatical error
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy voice - unshakeable confidence despite being wrong

jokes.text: No, I mean, if you were gonna go on a date with a woman, how would you want her to act? Like she was in a porn.
jokes.punchline: Frank giving the same answer to her clarification
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Frank's one-track mind perfectly executed

jokes.text: I want you to go out on your lunch hour and find something... in a women's clothing store.
jokes.punchline: The pause before specifying women's clothing store implies Liz shops elsewhere
jokes.explanation: Subtle dig at Liz's masculine fashion sense with perfect timing

jokes.text: You look like a fancy prostitute.
jokes.setup: Liz dressed up for her date
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that's actually an insult
jokes.explanation: Economical insult with perfect character voice for Josh

jokes.text: What up, Tra? This round, Texas Doozy. Face cards are wild, 3 is a jinx, 5s are 25.
jokes.punchline: Made-up poker variant with nonsensical rules
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor - treating made-up rules as legitimate

jokes.text: Well, then you best go home and put on your daddy's shoes, boy. This is a man's game.
jokes.setup: Writers wanting to play for quarters
jokes.punchline: Jack's over-the-top masculine posturing about poker stakes
jokes.explanation: Good Jack characterization - corporate alpha male extending to poker

jokes.text: Oh, my God, my wedding ring. Oh, my God. Oh, my God. My wife's gonna kill me!
jokes.punchline: Realizing he bet something irreplaceable with escalating panic
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Pete's desperation and poor judgment

jokes.text: Like, when Lutz here has a good hand, he stops eating.
jokes.punchline: Implication that Lutz is constantly eating except when focused
jokes.explanation: Efficient character humor - reveals Lutz's eating habits through poker analysis

jokes.text: Oh, really, is that what you think? Is that what I do? Am I doing that right now?
jokes.setup: Jack saying Frank asks inane questions when bluffing
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately doing exactly what Jack described
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-joke - Frank proves Jack's point by immediately demonstrating the behavior

jokes.text: Kenneth, how long you been sitting there? I'm taking Pete's place, he's in the bathroom, laying in his own sick.
jokes.setup: Kenneth suddenly appearing at poker table
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Pete's complete breakdown in bathroom
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Pete's misery with Kenneth's mysterious appearance

jokes.text: Oh, my God. He can't read Kenneth. He's a blank slate.
jokes.setup: Jack struggling to read Kenneth at poker
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's emptiness as a strategic advantage
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight - Kenneth's simplicity as accidental genius

jokes.text: I think I have what they refer to as a royal flush.
jokes.punchline: Formal, uncertain language about the best possible hand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth voice - polite uncertainty about obvious victory

jokes.text: Why would Jack just assume that we're lesbians? I am a lesbian.
jokes.punchline: Her date confirming the assumption was half right
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with economical delivery - subverts expectation efficiently

jokes.text: He approaches everything the same way... Locate the problem, isolate the problem, set the problem up with a lesbian.
jokes.punchline: Corporate speak escalating to absurd personal matchmaking
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke with perfect corporate parody structure

jokes.text: That's a pretty good joke for somebody from plastics. Well, I wasn't always in plastics. I used to work in water-process technologies, working mainly in primary metals. Oh, so you have a comedy background?
jokes.punchline: Liz sarcastically suggesting engineering backgrounds are comedic training
jokes.explanation: Good industry humor about corporate backgrounds and comedy writing

jokes.text: Your shoes. Well, I'm straight. Those shoes are definitely bi-curious.
jokes.setup: Liz asking what made Jack think she was gay
jokes.punchline: Jack doubling down on shoe-based sexuality assessment
jokes.explanation: Great Jack moment - applying sexual orientation to footwear with complete confidence

jokes.text: Did you know his middle name is Ellen? No, that's weird. Kenneth Ellen.
jokes.punchline: The feminine middle name repeated for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Sets up Kenneth's character quirks for later payoff

jokes.text: She thought you looked like Jennifer Jason Leigh. Really, she said that? Yes, I made her repeat it. I was sure she meant Jason Lee.
jokes.setup: Jack relaying a compliment about Liz's appearance
jokes.punchline: His surprise that she meant the female actor, not male
jokes.explanation: Good callback to masculine appearance theme with celebrity comparison twist

jokes.text: Kenneth, it is diamonds.
jokes.setup: Kenneth admiring how the light catches his poker winnings
jokes.punchline: Liz's deadpan correction about the obvious value
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment highlighting Kenneth's naivety

jokes.text: You are a puzzle, Kenneth Ellen. And I'm going to solve you.
jokes.setup: Jack's growing obsession with understanding Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Treating the page like a corporate challenge to be conquered
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack voice - corporate mindset applied to human relationships

jokes.text: Present-day Linda Ronstadt. No, Ruth Bader Ginsburg. Tootsie.
jokes.punchline: Each comparison getting less flattering and more masculine
jokes.explanation: Good escalation building on established theme of Liz's masculine appearance

jokes.text: It was a lady!
jokes.setup: Writers assuming her date went badly because of her flaws
jokes.punchline: The real reason revealed as gender mismatch
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection payoff with perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: You'd die, and they wouldn't find you until your neighbour's dog smelled you from the hallway.
jokes.punchline: Elaborating on the morbid scenario with specific details
jokes.explanation: Good callback that develops the dark humor theme

jokes.text: Ever since I turned 30, every time I get in or out of the bathtub, I think in my head, 'Careful, careful.'
jokes.punchline: Specific neurotic behavior about bathroom safety
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific observational humor about aging anxiety

jokes.text: Made out of shrimp and diving into a bowl of shrimp.
jokes.setup: Tracy commenting on Jack's catered shrimp display
jokes.punchline: Description of recursive shrimp sculpture
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist visual description - Tracy's stream of consciousness

jokes.text: So, hey, Dot Com, are we going to those three clubs tonight? You know, the doctor said I have four hearts in my body?
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to signal his entourage about poker cards
jokes.punchline: Random medical information as attempted code
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdism - treating nonsense as meaningful communication

jokes.text: All clovers. I win.
jokes.punchline: Using wrong terminology for suits with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - wrong but unstoppable

jokes.text: We're just friends, like Oprah and Gayle. Why is that so hard for everybody to believe?
jokes.punchline: Choosing example that everyone suspects is romantic
jokes.explanation: Great ironic reference that undermines her own argument

jokes.text: The guy obsessed with Charlie Chaplin. Neil. The one who played Halo under the name Slut Banger. Dennis. The gangly, red-haired guy who played guitar all the time. Conan.
jokes.punchline: Each boyfriend description getting worse, ending with Conan O'Brien
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect celebrity name-drop twist

jokes.text: What if I said to you, 'Go be with Frank now'? Ah, I would be honoured. Frank is a very tender, beautiful man.
jokes.punchline: Josh's sincere appreciation for Frank's qualities
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Josh's earnest response to hypothetical scenario

jokes.text: He's awesome. You can't read his thoughts 'cause he doesn't have any.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Frank's mental vacancy
jokes.explanation: Good callback that applies the 'blank slate' concept to Frank

jokes.text: Will you, little Kenneth Ellen Parcell from Stone Mountain, Georgia, growing up in your mama's tract house, dreaming of working on a TV show, dreaming of making it all the way to the NBC?
jokes.setup: Jack psychologically intimidating Kenneth at poker
jokes.punchline: Overly specific biographical details delivered as intimidation
jokes.explanation: Great Jack intimidation tactic - corporate psychology meets regional stereotyping

jokes.text: But you'll always be a pig farmer's son, boy, 'cause I smell fried bologna all over you.
jokes.punchline: Claiming to smell poverty/rural background on Kenneth
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's cruel corporate tactics

jokes.text: I've got a 2, a 4, a 9, a 6, and a king. That means I win.
jokes.setup: Kenneth revealing his terrible poker hand
jokes.punchline: Confidently declaring victory with nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - completely wrong but unshakeable confidence

jokes.text: Why? Because I believe that life is for the living. I believe in taking risks and biting off more than you can chew. And, also, people were yelling, and I got confused about the rules.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining his terrible poker decision
jokes.punchline: Philosophical wisdom undermined by admission of confusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kenneth moment - profound-sounding wisdom deflated by honest admission

jokes.text: The Italians have a saying, Lemon. 'Keep your friends close and your enemies closer.' And although they've never won a war or mass-produced a decent car, in this area, they are correct.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded acknowledgment of Italian wisdom while insulting their other achievements
jokes.explanation: Great Jack moment - casual xenophobia delivered with corporate authority

jokes.text: In five years, we'll all either be working for him... or be dead by his hand.
jokes.setup: Jack's assessment of Kenneth as threat
jokes.punchline: Treating the innocent page as potential murderer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jack's paranoia about Kenneth's hidden nature

jokes.text: So, unless you're ready to make a big life change, I need to move on, find my Stedman. I thought I was Oprah in this metaphor. Also, you're gay, so that's a little confusing. You should say, like, 'my lady Stedman.'
jokes.punchline: Liz getting distracted by metaphor logistics during breakup
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Liz's need to correct details during emotional moment

jokes.text: It's just, if I try to imagine us being together, I think, 'Yeah, OK, this could be fun.' You know, picking out furniture and making flowerbeds out of old railroad ties. But the thing is, if I'm gonna be with someone, it has to be a guy. You sound pretty sure about that. What can I say? I love a bald spot and a hairy back.
jokes.punchline: Specific appreciation for unattractive male features
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Liz's specific (low) standards for men

jokes.text: What if we say that in, like, 25 years, if neither of us has someone, we'll move in together and be roommates? And even though I am not into the sex stuff, if it helps you, I would let you do stuff to me.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to salvage friendship with pact
jokes.punchline: Offering awkward sexual compromise
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Liz's desperation and social awkwardness

jokes.text: That's funny. That's what the guys always say.
jokes.punchline: Liz noting pattern in her romantic rejections
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment tying together the episode's romantic failure theme

jokes.text: I can see your wedding ring. Idiot.
jokes.setup: Pete apparently hiding that he got his ring back
jokes.punchline: Someone calling out his obvious attempt at deception
jokes.explanation: Simple callback resolution showing Pete got his ring back but poorly concealing it
